Moonstone Ring  
 


Slot: FINGER
STR: +3 MANA: +30
Focus: Extended Range II
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)
Moonstone Ring

Slot: FINGER
STR: +3 MANA: +30
Focus: Extended Range II
WT: 0.1 Size: TINY
Class: ALL
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 7 (General: Group)

Item Type:Armor
Stackable:No
Merchant Value:8 pp 9 gp 0 sp 0 cp
Tribute:90
Submitted By:Doriath
Lucy Entry By:Kerasota
Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2022-01-19 04:48:03
Page Updated:Wed Apr 12th, 2017

Expansion: Original
Average Price: No Data Pricing Data...
Rarity: Common
Level to Attain: 42
[Drops | Comments ]

Drops

This item is found on creatures.

Lower Guk
NPC Name
a frenzied ghoul - Lower Guk
The Fabled Frenzied Ghoul


Zone(s) Found In:


Zone Name
Lower Guk


Uploaded November 27th, 2008
Send a correction
Post Comment
Moonstone Ring
QuoteReply
# May 04 2008 at 6:40 AM Rating: Good
snailish
4,580 posts
Confirmed drop is the frenzied ghoul in Lower Guk. Most downloaded maps have the frenzied labelled on them if you are not familiar with Lower Guk.

Guild tribute offers 90 points.

Guild hall vendor offered 8p.

This remains an excellent item for any caster under level 44 that doesn't have extended range focus from another gear slot.
▲ Page top
Fine for Clerics too
QuoteReply
# Mar 12 2004 at 6:35 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591799280781
955 posts
my Cleric 22nd acquired it yesterday in the Bazaar (MR)and what more can I want than +3 STR for carrying my armor, +30 mana to start with and an extended range on root and pacify for 50p? =)
▲ Page top
Bard
QuoteReply
# Jan 31 2004 at 6:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591799343864
16 posts
Does this ring work for any bard songs, aoe's really is what I'm wondering. Its worth the slot if I can kite mobs an extra foot away from hairy back.
▲ Page top
RE: Bard
QuoteReply
# Feb 15 2004 at 12:50 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592759721414
6 posts
No focus effect of any kind works for bards. Songs cannot be enhanced at all.
▲ Page top
RE: Bard
QuoteReply
# Feb 23 2004 at 12:28 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591756893428
Scholar
78 posts
That's not entirely correct.

Bards use their instruments to give their songs more power... ie using a lute to give Hymn of Restoration more regen power, or a drum to boost DoT damage.

However, the only way to gain range on songs is to invest in the Extended Notes AA, and even then, it won't work on most detrimental songs.
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 24 2003 at 1:09 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) if i saw one selling for 10pp i wouldnt buy it...i wouldnt use this junk to twink a level 1
RE: these rings suck
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2003 at 10:50 AM Rating: Good
__DEL__1594570664193
Scholar
245 posts
The Moonstone Ring is an excellent, inexpensive item for:

(1) Casters and hybrids who use level 44 spells and lower for pulls.

(2) Casters and hybrids who use level 44 spells and lower to kite, or who like to root and nuke, or otherwise cast from a distance.

As has been noted in other posts, you will recieve a message when the ring allows you to cast a spell on a mob that would otherwise have been out of range. Combine this with a focus effect item that hastens your damage spell, and you often have time to lands two spells on the mob before it can reach you.

Kamgar
▲ Page top
RE: these rings suck
QuoteReply
# Apr 11 2003 at 2:24 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1591772632869
89 posts
So the extra 3 str to carry items is junk eh? Maybe...
So the extra 30 mana for a level 1-10 is junk eh?
So the extended range to level 44 spells is junk eh?

Ever try a wizard with a bolt spell? 360 feet is more impressive when the mob dies at your feet after you get the third shot off... touch me? YOU THOUGHT!

I would LOVE to have this ring on my little wizzie instead of my stupid +2 INT ring... BAH!

Edited, Fri Apr 11 02:47:47 2003
▲ Page top
Does this effect work?
QuoteReply
# Sep 13 2002 at 11:44 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255037618
231 posts
I have 2 moonstone rings as well and I don't notice anything. You wouldn't beleive how close I have to get to mobs just to cast tash on them!
▲ Page top
RE: Does this effect work?
QuoteReply
# Jun 05 2003 at 6:33 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256983678
53 posts
There's a standard technique used by many casters which will show you that this item works, and should show you how useful it is to you:

Do this first without the ring on.

Find the camp you propose to hunt (fixed camp, single pull if you are soloing).

Stand in range, target mob, cast your pull spell. Before it goes off, interrupt it and move back a pace or two.

Repeat until you find the exact point at which you are just in range.

Pull and kill.

Now put on the ring and move back, casting and interrupting, until you find the new maximum range.

Pull and kill.

If the extra time the longer range allows means you can get off an extra, useful spell then it's worth it. If not, then maybe you could find a better finger slot item.

▲ Page top
RE: Does this effect work?
QuoteReply
# Feb 21 2003 at 4:33 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592255681203
26 posts
i have one of these on my 39 pally and it works for me just fine. You can tell when the effect is triggered by a messing saying something like your moonstone ring begins to shine or something like that even though i just got it for the str and mana boost.

__DEL__1592249265948

this would be good for mages and wizards also with the 300 range bolt spells. you range just went out to 345. thats about enough time to get three bolts on the mob at maximum range and pretty much kill it. I can easily get 2 bolts off before something gets to me with 300 range. Sadly i am 54 now and Scars of sigil is not effected by this ring. 2 second casting time and 345 range would have been sweet. Wish they had focus effect when I was younger

# Dec 31 2001 at 1:09 PM Rating: Good
__DEL__1592249542030
36 posts
Why does everyone automatically assume this is only good for low levels? As a hybrid, 35 Ranger for the moment--this is still not a bad ring. Everyone makes it sound like as a 35 Ranger I should be wearing full Forrest Stalker. That is BS. Just enjoy the game--for a 35 Ranger, if you earned it--it is good for you. Get real here, not everyone has a level 60 prime to get twink gear.

# Oct 22 2001 at 8:12 PM Rating: Default

i just picked this up in EC for 50pp for two. there not a bad help for my pally. the +6 str and +60 comes in handy. there alot better then the drev rings i had on.
▲ Page top
Pretty nice.
QuoteReply
# Oct 14 2001 at 1:44 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765525919
Scholar
748 posts
I admit that these aren't that great for the level of the people that can easily get these. But they're great for my level 12 enchanter. I was in Lower Guk today with my main, holding the Magi room with people (32 is barely enough to do this, I was getting beat up quite a lot!) and got killed horribly. After being rezzed, and before being escorted out since a level 51 joined and I wasn't getting exp, they let me roll for the adamantite band that dropped. I actually won the roll, nice becuase I almost never do, and gave me the choice between the adamantite or a PAIR of moonstones. Adamantite has nice hit points, but negative magic resistance, and these were great for my enchanter, so I picked these. A great reward for enduring an area I shouldn't have been in yet! Lower Guk's got great loot, will definitely revisit the pace in a few more levels.
▲ Page top
very good

Heeroyuy Flowersniffer

Vallon Zek
